title: Copy trading works. The problem is you’ve only seen the scammers.
author: Responsible_Fill_180
selftext:
I started offering copy trading for free about 10 months ago. No upfront fees, no subscriptions. Just a small group of 22 people who decided to trust me.

The deal is simple: they only pay when they make a profit. No profit, no payment. That’s it.

I’m not getting rich off this. The returns aren’t crazy — 20-30% of the invested amount per month on average. But it’s consistent and stable. No huge pumps, no devastating dumps. Just slow, boring, reliable growth.

I know copy trading has a bad reputation. A lot of people got burned by scammers promising 10x in a week or exit scammers who disappear with the funds. And honestly? I get the distrust. If I hadn’t seen this work with my own eyes over 10 months, I might not believe it either.

But for those who actually run serious copy trading — the ones who take it as a responsibility, not a quick cash grab — you know exactly what I’m talking about. It *can* work. The problem is bad actors ruined trust for everyone.

Not writing this to recruit anyone or prove a point. Just sharing that it’s possible to do this transparently. If more people ran it like this (no upfront fees, pay only on real profit, realistic targets), maybe fewer people would get scammed.
